entry.title: build a Webserver with a high-available, scalable environment
entry.content:
735 AWS project - AnyGroupLLC

VPC design

Our VPC design uses this CIDR block (10.0.0.0/16) and runs across two Availability Zones (us-east-1a, us-east-1b) for better availability.

Each AZ has one public subnet and three private subnets for the frontend, backend, and database. There is a internet gateway attached to this VPC, and a endpoint S3 gateway for private instance to access resource.

Subnets

The public subnets are used for the NAT instances and web-ALB,

while the private subnets are used for EC2 instances for web, app, and database services.

All these subnets are corresponding to our design schema for CIDR block setting.

Route tables

The public route table directs traffic to the Internet Gateway for internet access, and route local traffic to other VPC resources.

The private route table AZ1 are used for private subnets in AZ1, routing traffic to the NAT instance’s network interface, and S3 Gateway Endpoint, so private instances can reach Amazon S3 resource through the AWS network instead of the internet. and also local traffic to other VPC resources.

The private route table AZ2 are used for private subnets in AZ2. The only difference with the private route table 1 is, the target of the NAT instance, having a different network interface id.

Security Groups

The web-ALB group allows inbound HTTP from the internet.

The web group accepts HTTP traffic from the web-ALB group.

The app-ALB group receives traffic from the web group.

And the app group only accepts traffic from that app-ALB group.

The database group only accepts traffic from the app group.

The NAT group allows all other traffic from the VPC and SSH from my IP, so NAT instance can also act as a Bastion host.

NACLs

For Network ACLs, all inbound traffic was open for public subnets.

for private subnets, only traffic from VPC are allowed.

NAT instances

We used NAT instances instead of NAT Gateways to save cost, following the Cost Optimization pillar.

We run an ec2 instance and using SSH connecting to the instance, after setting the network forwarding, the instance can act as a NAT.

Also, the web security group needs to allow SSH traffic from NAT security group.

Each AZ has its own NAT instance, so NAT instance are put in public subnets, assigned with a public IP.

For private route tables in each AZ, they point to different network interfaces of NAT instances, therefore directing traffic from instance in private subnets, through NAT instance, to the internet.

EC2

We use EC2 instance to run the web server and app server,

here we choose the t3.micro instance for demonstrating,

in the production environment, we can change to t3.medium for web instance, and t3.xlarge for app instance. which would be more suitable for the CPU, and memory requirements.

For web instance, we choose the Linux system as required, and we set up a web server with userdata to install and start the web service. This web instance run in private subnet for frontend.

For app instance, we choose the Windows system as required, and also install and run the example service by userdata script. This app instance run in private subnet for backend.

AMIs

And after these two instance running well, we create image from these instance, preparing for the launch template for auto-scaling group for web and app layers.

Target Groups

We create web target group and app target group for recording the instance from web and app ASG, and serving for their health check.

Application Load Balancers

For availability,

we create ALB for both web and app layers, the web-ALB assigned with public subnets, therefore providing the internet access.

Here we can use this web address to access our simple web page, which support our web server successfully running and the outside traffic can access to the web instance by the web-ALB.

And this web-ALB listens on port 80 HTTP, only healthy instance can serve for requests. The web-ALB will forward the request to the healthy instance in web target-group.

And the app-ALB is used for receiving requests from the web instances, then forwarding to app target-group.

Auto-Scaling Groups

For scalability,

We create auto-scaling group for both web and app layers.

Frontend-ASG connect to the web-ALB, using launch template for web instance and AMI created by the web server, and register instance to web target-group.

Backend-ASG connect to the app-ALB, using launch template for app instance and AMI created by the app server, and register instance to app target-group.

For reliability,

we enable health checking on ASG to support shutting down unhealthy instance and creating new instance automatically,

and also enable monitoring on each instance to check the their status.

Here we just set desired capacity as 1, minimum as 1, and maximum as 2 for demonstrating and saving budgets.

In production environment, we can adjust the capacity as we need.
entry.summary: 735

entry.title: Commands for exporting resources on AWS Learner Lab
entry.content: Commands for exporting resources on AWS Learner Lab

entry.title: Creating Maintainable Software
entry.content:
Creating Maintainable Software

  1. 可维护性的三大支柱

可理解性 (Comprehensibility/Analysability)：快速准确地理解代码的实现和意图。

可修改性 (Alterability/Modifiability)：高效地对产品进行更改，而不引入缺陷或降低现有质量。

可测试性 (Testability)：有效率地建立和执行测试的能力。

  1. 程序理解模型 (PCM)

理解开发者如何通过外部表示（代码、文档）和自身知识（知识库）构建对程序的心理模型。

所有的代码质量实践，其最终目标都是优化 PCM 中的同化过程，减少理解成本。

  1. 面向对象设计的维护性基础

Context Schema（上下文图式）：关注问题域的概念，类设计应与其匹配 。

Encapsulation（封装）：确保对象行为与所代表的概念一致 。

Abstraction（抽象）：类应是对现实或设计概念的良好抽象 。

  1. 代码结构和质量原则 (CQP)

Code Quality Principles (CQP)：提供了一个通用的框架来解释和应用编程约定 。

DRY (Don’t Repeat Yourself)：避免代码重复，重复代码会增加阅读量和维护风险 。

命名原则：使用意图揭示的名称，避免模糊和误导性的名称，以提高可理解性 。

  1. 函数和类的设计原则

  • 函数 (Functions)：

    • “One Thing”：函数应只做一件事 。

    • 统一抽象级别：函数中所有语句都应在同一抽象级别上 。

    • 参数数量：尽量减少参数数量，或使用 Argument Object 来封装相关参数 。

  • 类 (Classes)：

    • 单一职责原则 (SRP)：一个类应只有一个修改的理由 。SRP 是提高可修改性的核心。

    • 高内聚 (High Cohesion)：类中的内部元素应紧密相关地执行同一任务 。

  1. 代码异味与重构实践 (Unencapsulated Collection)

代码异味：Unencapsulated Collection（未封装的集合）是一个重要的设计问题 。

重构方法：通过创建一个集合类，将集合设为私有字段，并将所有针对该集合的业务逻辑移动到该类的方法中，从而实现封装 。

重构收益：显著提高内聚性、增强封装、降低变更风险
entry.summary: <h1 id="Creating-Maintainable-Software"><a href="#Creating-Maintainable-Software" class="headerlink" title="Creating Maintainable Software">

entry.title: Creating Maintainable Software - midterm review
entry.content:
Creating Maintainable Software

Better maintainability → lower maintenance cost → higher overall software quality.

Maintenance:

  • corrective - remove defects
  • adaptive - change the product to a changed environment
  • preventative - improving some aspect of its quality
  • perfective - improve some aspect of its usefulness, including adding new features

Maintainability

definition: The degree of effectiveness and efficiency with which maintenance activities can be carried out on the product.

Maintenance costs dominate the lifetime cost of a software product. The more maintainable the software, the lower the maintenance costs, and so the lower the lifetime cost.

Each part of Maintenance is independent. (E.G. some code is “hard” to understand but “easy” to change)

maintenance activities:

  • comprehensibility (analysability) : implementation can be understood.
  • alterability (modifiability) : system can be changed without introducing defects.
  • testability : test criteria can be established.

​

Comprehensibility

PCM

Program Comprehension Model

  • Knowledge base

    • context schema - > domain knowledge

    • design schema -> knowledge of programming language

    • Mental model : current understanding of the code

  • Assimilation Process : use information to update Mental model to understand the program

  • External Representation : code, documents …

How to use PCM

Figure out the occasion affects which links between these parts.

Comprehensibility depends on what is in KB, what is available in the ER, how efficient the assimilation process is.

Alterability

Change Cases: specifying what needs to change:

  • likelihood (the case will occur)
  • potential impact
    • to the requirements, to the behavior of existing system

Assess the alterability of the design

Focus on change cases with high likelihood and high impact

(lower likelihood, lower impact -> less affect on alterability assessment)

  • change one… have less work than multiple…
    • class, method, statement
  • simple work have less work than complex work

less need to change -> more alterable

  • fewer places to deal with -> efficiency
  • less likely to make mistakes -> effectiveness

Testability

Testing Scopes

  • unit testing - single class or subsystem (small number of classes)
  • integration testing - combination of subsystems
  • system testing - the whole system

Test Case

a set of actions performed on the Implementation Under Test (IUT) to determine if it results in the correct behavior

  • pretest state -> inputs -> expected state or behavior

Test Criteria

requirements on a set of test cases must pass

improving testability : controllability, observability -> more classes, methods -> automate

Naming

using PCM to determine better name: easy to add to MM > easily found in KB > avoid going to ER

Meaningful Names

  • Intention-Revealing : the name can explain its function without comments
  • Avoid Encoding : don’t use special prefix or name styles for different types

Code Quality Principles (CQP)

Code Style Mode

Clear Layout -> Clear Presentation

  • clear layout between different elements:
    • characters, space, indentations, brackets, line length

Explanatory Language

  • clear intent and meaning: documentation, names(variables, functions), language feature(annotations)
  • don’t reuse for different purposes within the same scope

Simple Constructs

  • minimize nesting
  • easy flow of control (with few breaks and exceptions)
  • easy expression

Be Consistent

  • consistent implementation for similar jobs
  • using language idiom (documentation, notation) consistently

Avoid Duplication -> Single Definition

  • using loops or methods, classes instead of repeated codes

No Unused Content -> Contributing Content

  • remove unreachable code, redundant branches, unused documentation

Modular Structure

  • placing related code together with a clear task
  • minimal dependence on other modules, and the scope of constants and variables
  • functions aim to a single task, minimum parameters

Congruent Implementation

  • implementation (structure, process) are consistent with the nature of the problem

Maintainable Functions

structured programming: breaks things into separately-callable units

  • small

    • decomposed a large function into several smaller functions

  • do one thing <- identify duplication

    • reduce revisiting

  • explain yourself in code

    • use small function to create “self-documenting” code
    • avoid use of literal values

  • one level of abstraction per function -> small

    • avoid “high level” and “low level” concepts in the same function
    • reduce number of levels of abstraction

  • reading code from top to bottom -> order of functions ▽

    • the first function is the most abstract, call to other

  • use descriptive names <- the one thing it does

  • reduce number of parameters

    • Wrap some of the existing arguments into a class

  • reduce returns

  • have no side effects

Classes

  • Organisation
    • order: constants -> other static -> instance -> constructors -> public methods -> non public methods
  • Encapsulation -> object behavior is consistent with the concept it represents
  • Small
  • Single Responsibility Principle (SRP)
  • Cohesion

Class Quality

  • abstraction (matches a concept from the context or design schema)
  • single responsibility principle
  • cohesion : fields associated with methods (rather than using a few fields in methods)

Tools for comprehending programs

  • program analysis tools

    • AST (abstract syntax tree), identify potential quality issues

  • software visualization tools <- limited evidence shows helps

    • software metrics (number of classes), relationships between elements

  • program languages

  • PMD - linter to detect

  • NcssCount (Non-commenting Source Statements)

  • ControlStatementBraces

  • FieldVisibility : fields should not accessible from other classes

  • Visualizations of object creation

  • Automated “Concept” Extraction (ACE)

    • things to recognise
      • Design (and Architecture) Patterns — common structure organizations
      • Abstractions — high-level ideas (typically from problem)
      • Across Abstractions — characteristics that occur across abstractions
      • Across Packages — characteristics that occur across packages
    • Threat to Validity: Not use meaningful names in a consistent manner
    • Benefits to understanding: Seeing dependencies that between different concepts
    • Extraction Process
      • divide names into component -> keywords
      • look for classes at the top of inheritance corresponding to keywords
      • look for keywords occur in multiple places, corresponding to patterns

  • UML (Unified Modeling Language)

a visual language used to describe software: class, sequence, use case, deployment

  • Remix Programming language

use standard compiler technology to make code readable
entry.summary: <h1 id="Creating-Maintainable-Software"><a href="#Creating-Maintainable-Software" class="headerlink" title="Creating Maintainable Software">

entry.title: Security Group and NACL
entry.content:
Security Group

  • Associated with instance
  • Stateful: allow return traffic
  • default rule:
    • allow all out-bound traffic
    • deny all in-bound traffic
  • Only allow traffic defined by explicit rules
  • Use cases: controlling access to specific instances (application access)

Network ACL

  • Associated with subnets
  • Stateless: not allow return traffic
  • Default ACL for VPC
    • allow all in-bound and out-bound traffic
  • Self-defined ACL default:
    • deny all in-bound and out-bound traffic
  • Use cases: controlling traffic bound of the subnets (network protecting, compliance, traffic control, IP blocking)
entry.summary:
Security Group

  • Associated with

entry.title: Record of debuging on AWS Learner Lab
entry.content:
AWS Learner Lab 环境 Debug 记录

问题背景

ALB 连接的前端 target group 中的 server instance 健康检查状态一直是 unhealthy: request timed out 504，无法访问到网页内容

问题分析

504 表示请求已经发给了服务器但是服务器处理超时没有返回。那么 ALB -> priavte subnet 的通道应该是正常的。
服务器健康检查失败的原因有可能是 check path 或者端口跟 ALB 设置不一致，经检查也不是这个问题。
那就只可能是 server 服务没有启动，该服务器没有在正常监听处理请求导致的。

初步措施

给 ALB 的 Target group 对应的 AutoScaling Group Launch template 加上 userdata，在 instance 初始化时安装并启动 nginx 服务。但是操作之后，依然健康检查失败。只能连上 instance 去查看 nginx 服务到底有没有启动了，但是由于 instance 在 private subnet，还需要经过 public subnet 的跳板机才能进行 ssh 连接。

NAT instance 和 跳板机 配置

为了节省费用，创建了一个 EC2 同时用作 NAT instance 和 Bastion 跳板机。
首先创建 AmazonLinux2023 EC2 实例， Security Group 选 NAT security group，放在 public-subnet，记得勾选 keypair - vocky 以便进行 ssh 连接。

NAT instance 的 network interface 设置

NAT network interface 中的 source/dest check 必须去掉 enable，不然 AWS 会丢弃所有不是 NAT 自己发出的包（包括你私网 EC2 的出站包）。
由于 AWS Learner Lab 在实验超时或者暂停之后会关闭所有 EC2 instance，所有在 lab 重启之后，如果 NAT instance 被重置，上面操作就要重复进行。

NAT instance 设置

  • NAT security group
    inbound: ssh | my-ip
    Inbound: all traffic | VPC CIDR
  • Frontend security group
    Outbound: All traffic | Destination: 0.0.0.0/0

private subnet route table -> nat instance
lab 重启之后 NAT instance 如果被重置就要重复操作， network interface 会变

SSH 连接 NAT instance

连上 instance 之后 执行以下脚本 ( lab 重启之后得重新执行)

通过跳板机 SSH 连接 private subnet instance

用 putty 连接 NAT instance 当跳板机连接 private subnet:

PuTTY → “Connection → SSH → Auth → Allow agent forwarding”
在 Windows 上启动 Pageant（PuTTY 自带的 SSH agent）并加载 vockey.ppk。
这样登录 NAT 时，NAT 会“借用”你本机的密钥去认证私网 EC2，不需要上传密钥。
登录 NAT 后直接执行：ssh ec2-user@10.0.XX.XXX

连上 ec2 之后

排除完网络问题, 进一步分析

解决了 private subnet 的网络问题之后，还是安装不了 nginx，怀疑是 learner lab 环境下官方 S3 repo 仓库源无法访问。
(因为 NAT instance 是可以安装 nginx 的，NAT 实例本身在 Public Subnet，直接连 Internet Gateway，所以它的出网流量走的是公网路由，没有经过 Learner Lab 的 NAT Egress 限制)

备选解决方案

  1. 在 NAT instance 自建 repo proxy 反代，把流量重定向
  2. Chatgpt 说 换成 Amazon Linux 2 AMI 源 Leaner lab 就允许了？？(不知道可不可信但这个贵)

方案 A - NAT 反代

NAT 执行以下命令

private subnet instance 执行以下命令

注意这里的 ip address 要改成 NAT instance 实际的 private ip

理论上这些命令加到 NAT instance 和 private subnet instance 的 userdata 中应该就可以在创建时自行完成 nginx 安装和启动，但是实际上操作时还是没法自动完成，原因还有待进一步排查。（不过基本上问题都是由于 Learner Lab 的网络和权限限制以及重启资源造成的）

最新进展

因为 vpc 中是有 endpoint 的，所以正常不需要 NAT 去做反代也应该能访问到 S3 源。反而是因为 userdata 中安装 nginx 的命令执行太早，在网络服务等还没初始化好的情况下就执行失败了，所以才会出现后续手动连接上去能安装成功但是启动时却总是失败的问题。
另一个发现是 EC2 连不同 AZ 的 NAT 会出问题，没办法正常访问外网，所以还是要分开两个 NAT，分别设置两个路由表

NAT instance 设置

NAT Security group 设置

inbound: ssh | my-ip
Inbound: all traffic | VPC CIDR

Frontend security group 设置

Outbound: All traffic | Destination: 0.0.0.0/0

Private subnet Route table 设置

0.0.0.0/0 -> nat instance id
lab 重启之后要检查是否要改， 因为 network interface 有可能会变

web userdata 内容

app userdata 内容
entry.summary: AWS L

entry.title: Hello World
entry.content:
Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub.

Quick Start

Create a new post

More info: Writing

Run server

More info: Server

Generate static files

More info: Generating

Deploy to remote sites

More info: Deployment
entry.summary: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for
